general_information: Candidate is transitioning from chemical/process engineering to software engineering. Currently working part-time as a software engineer at AddSalt (a AI receptionist for restaurants and event spaces) while completing an M.S. in Computer Science at Georgia Tech (online program, part-time alongside full-time Chevron role). Targeting full-time software engineering positions with more interest in backend and full stack work. School work has included course work in supervised learning, clustering and dimension reduction, neural nets (with pytorch) and reinforcement learning. Other courses have included operating systems with building a basic file transfer protocol and distributed file system.

experience_items.text: Built a LangGraph subagent in Python to handle phone number collection and verification, enabling reservation bookings from landline callers previously unsupported by the system.

Designed and implemented a skill-based call routing system in Go enabling an AI assistant to dynamically forward calls based on caller intent, delivering new product capability that opened access to tenss of new customers
Built a multi-assistant system allowing multiple AI assistants per phone number and location, enabling time-based switching for hundreds of locations handling thousands of calls per day.

Built an OAuth 2.0 service in Go handling token exchange, in-memory caching, and automatic refresh — enabling a new reservation platform integration and unlocking potential for hundreds of new locations across customers.
Built a call log dashboard (React + Go) integrating third-party APIs to surface call recordings and transcripts, used by both internal team and restaurant staff for customer support analysis.
Built customer-facing configuration pages in React with Go backend services, including org-level user management and agent version toggling.
Developed technical proposals supporting 5 catalyst sales worth $5-10M each, modeling expected performance to support client decision-making

Served as primary technical point of contact for 5 refinery clients, diagnosing performance anomalies and identifying optimizations that helped clients hit production targets and improve yield. Contributed to contract renewals and expanded engagements.
Automated chart generation and report packaging with VBA scripts for monthly client performance reports, tracking degradation trends and flagging operational deviations across 5 refinery clients
Supported on-schedule commissioning of 6 refinery units at domestic and international client sites, validating live system performance against design models during startup.

Contributed to 3 engineering design packages (one as lead engineer) for $300-500M refinery capital projects, building and validating process simulation models to support technical decisions and client deliverables.
Supervised 3 engineers on a design package, coordinating across disciplines and catching significant errors through technical review that would have required costly rework.
Traveled to client site to validate as-built construction against design specs, identifying significant discrepancies that would have required major rework if left undetected.
Diagnosed root causes across multiple production systems and implemented fixes saving $800K+ — by improving system capacity, reducing equipment downtime, and avoiding production curtailments.
Resolved a month-long propane product quality incident by developing short- and long-term fixes to restore sales continuity. Built an automated monitoring tool to prevent recurrence.

Led process engineering scope for a $200M refinery turnaround 2-year planning horizon through 60-day execution. Responsibilities included owning scope definition, vendor coordination, key technical decisions, and leading 2 other engineers during execution.

Conducted multi-week root cause investigation of a complex process anomaly, analyzing historical data and equipment constraints to develop recommendations presented to and accepted by management. Implemented temporary fix to restore 5% of production capacity.
